title: VCP Environment Attestation
description: Attests WHAT AN ACTOR IS — that a Gateway, Provider, or Agent runs the genuine code it claims, in the environment it claims (SPEC §27). Distinct from the result attestation of §9 (which attests what a call did). Off by default; produced only when a manifest sets effects.requires_attestation or policy returns an `attest` obligation. Attest-once / reference-many: the per-call envelope carries only an attestation_ref, never this full statement.

tier.description: statement = signed build/digest claim (no special hardware; L2/L3). tee = hardware remote-attestation evidence per RFC 9334 (L4, see RFC 0008).

issuer.description: DID/URI of the attesting actor; its key signs this statement.

build_digest.description: Claimed source/build digest; SHOULD match manifest provenance (RFC 0002).

measurement.description: OPTIONAL platform measurement (e.g. PCR digest / TEE quote hash) for tier=tee.

boot_epoch.description: Stable per-boot/session identifier; the Gateway caches the verified result keyed by (issuer key, boot_epoch).

nonce.description: Gateway-issued challenge this statement is bound to (freshness / anti-replay, §27.4).

evidence_ref.description: For tier=tee: reference to full RATS Evidence appraised out-of-band.
